# Arts & Culture — 1777

Literature, music, and cultural works of 1777.

<!-- BEGIN GENERATED: category-index — maintained by build-structure; do not edit by hand -->
| Topic | Description |
|---|---|
| [Gluck's Armide](gluck-armide.md) | Christoph Willibald Gluck's final Paris opera premiered at the Académie Royale de Musique on September 23, 1777, setting Quinault's 1686 libretto and inflaming the Gluckistes–Piccinnistes controversy. |
| [Haydn's Il mondo della luna](haydn-il-mondo-della-luna.md) | Joseph Haydn's opera buffa on Goldoni's libretto premiered at Eszterháza on August 3, 1777 for Count Anton Esterházy's wedding, a landmark dramma giocoso later lost and rediscovered. |
| [The School for Scandal](sheridan-school-for-scandal.md) | Richard Brinsley Sheridan's comedy of manners premiered at Drury Lane on May 8, 1777, becoming a landmark of English comedy and the bridge between Restoration wit and the comic drama of Wilde and Shaw. |
| [Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart's 1777 European Tour](mozart-1777-tour.md) | Mozart broke from the Salzburg court and toured Munich, Augsburg, and Mannheim from September 1777, completing the "Jenamy" Concerto K. 271 and Flute Quartet K. 285 and absorbing the Mannheim orchestral style. |
<!-- END GENERATED: category-index -->
